products.name: Spectre
products.descriptor: Advanced tethered UAS for multi-payload ISR and communications relay

evidence.snippet:
Spectre
=======
THE ONLY DIU BLUE LIST CERTIFIED TETHERED UAS
---------------------------------------------
The Most Advanced Tethered Drone
--------------------------------
Purpose-built for radios and engineered for versatility, SPECTRE is the most advanced tethered drone available today. Augment and elevate your mission with greater payload capacity, multi-payload capabilities, AI-integrations, and persistent network extension.

Hoverfly Spectre is the first and only Tethered UAS platform cleared for the DIU Blue UAS List and verified as secure and NDAA compliant through AUVSI Green UAS.
[BLUE LIST](https://www.diu.mil/blue-uas-cleared-list) [GREEN LIST](https://www.auvsi.org/news/auvsi-announces-green-uas-certification-for-hoverfly-spectre-tethered-drone/)

products.name: Sentry
products.descriptor: Static high-endurance tethered UAS for persistent surveillance and perimeter protection

evidence.snippet:
SENTRY
------
Sentry is the blueprint for Tethered Drone innovation.
With an unparalleled legacy of deployments, this single-payload TeUAS is fielded and maintained by our armed forces and has evolved into a critical infrastructure asset for the Integrated Tactical Network.
[Learn More](/tethered-drones-uas/sentry/)

products.name: HIVE RECON
products.descriptor: Persistent ISR tethered drone configuration for continuous elevated overwatch

evidence.snippet:
### RECON
Persistent ISR
Maintain a constant elevated vantage point with leading sensors and AI-vision to ensure uninterrupted overwatch and situational awareness across critical mission zones.
[Learn More](https://hoverflytech.com/hive/recon/)

products.name: HIVE SPEARHEAD
products.descriptor: Vehicle/vessel-mountable tethered drone for on-the-move ISR and secure overwatch

evidence.snippet:
### SPEARHEAD
On-The-Move
Mountable to vehicles or vessels, SPEARHEAD enables live ISR during movement for real-time intelligence and secure overwatch in mobile environments.
[Learn More](https://hoverflytech.com/hive/spearhead/)

products.name: HIVE RELAY
products.descriptor: Variable height antenna tethered drone extending tactical comms to 200 feet

evidence.snippet:
### RELAY
Variable Height Antenna (VHA)
Extend tactical communications to 200 feet, creating a persistent, elevated comms bubble that keeps command agile and securely connected.
[Learn More](https://hoverflytech.com/hive/relay/)

products.name: HIVE SHIELD
products.descriptor: Live overwatch tethered drone for continuous security and event surveillance

evidence.snippet:
### SHIELD
Live Overwatch Drone
Deliver continuous live-streaming for security and event monitoring with seamless integration into SIEM and existing surveillance infrastructure.
[Learn More](/hive/shield/)

products.name: HIVE NEXUS
products.descriptor: Unmanned teaming tethered drone expanding command range up to 10x via elevated network extension

evidence.snippet:
### NEXUS
Unmanned Teaming
Interconnect unmanned systems to multiply ISR and strike capabilities while expanding command range up to 10x via elevated network extension.
[Learn More](/hive/nexus/)

products.name: Drone as a Service (DAAS)
products.descriptor: Managed tethered drone operations service for defense, security, and public safety missions

unresolved_questions: Sentry dedicated product page (https://hoverflytech.com/tethered-drones-uas/sentry) was skipped — full specs, payload options, and explicit NDAA/Blue UAS status not confirmed.

All five HIVE sub-product pages (RECON, SPEARHEAD, RELAY, SHIELD, NEXUS) were skipped_class_budget; descriptions are sourced only from brief nav/ecosystem blurbs on other pages — granularity, readiness, and compliance status could not be confirmed.
DAAS page (https://hoverflytech.com/services/drone-as-a-service-daas) was skipped — nature of the service offering, pricing model, and any compliance claims unknown.

HIVE products classified based on secondary page descriptions only; they appear to be mission-configured packages of the underlying Sentry/Spectre platforms rather than separate physical UAS — this interpretation may need revision if dedicated pages reveal distinct hardware.
